channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Vår Energi to acquire BlueNord in $1.3bn cash-and-stock deal
item.description: Vår Energi has agreed to acquire rival Norwegian oil and gas company BlueNord in a cash and stock deal worth Nkr12.84bn ($1.33bn).

item.title: China's Oil Output Hits Record High
item.description: China produced a record amount of crude oil last year, at 216 million tons, with natural gas production also rising substantially, the National Energy Administration reported today. Together, the oil and gas output of…

item.title: Surging Oil Prices Push Japan’s Import Bill to an Unprecedented High
item.description: Surging oil prices have led to a significant increase in Japan’s import bill, hitting an all-time high last month of $89.46 billion, Reuters has reported, citing government data. The June total was 25.4% higher than a…

item.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Threatens Long-Term Energy Shock Across Asia
item.description: The impact of the war between the United States and Iran on oil-importing nations in Asia could last years or even decades because of these countries’ heavy dependence on those imports, energy analyst Vandana Hari of…

item.title: Oil Prices Climb as U.S.-Iran Conflict Shows No Signs of Slowing
item.description: Oil prices continued to climb in early Asian trade on Wednesday as the renewed hostilities between the United States and Iran showed no signs of slowing. At the time of writing, Brent crude was trading at $92.44, up…

item.title: China's Next Move Could Decide Where Oil Prices Go This Year
item.description: China’s demand for crude oil imports and the pace of its refined products exports will help shape the trend in oil prices through the end of the year, alongside the supply disruptions in the Middle East. Decade-low…

item.title: Fossil Fuels Still Generate 57% of the World's Electricity
item.description: When the Energy Institute reported in its Statistical Review of World Energy that the global grid is still ruled by hydrocarbons, it may have come as a surprise to some observers. Now, another report has confirmed the…

item.title: US Crude Oil Inventories Build As Hormuz Shipping Headache Drags On
item.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States rose by 2.603 million barrels in the week ending July 17. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories fell by 564,000…

item.title: Energy IPOs Are Booming Thanks to AI’s Insatiable Thirst for Power
item.description: The artificial intelligence boom has created an era of unprecedented uncertainty in the global economy. Large language models are evolving as quickly as they are being integrated, creating a major headache for anyone…

item.title: Norway's Oil Output Beats Forecasts as Hormuz Traffic Stays Near Crisis Lows
item.description: Norway's crude oil production beat official forecasts in June as offshore fields returned from seasonal maintenance, delivering a stronger-than-expected increase in supply at a time when global markets remain focused on…

item.title: Kazakhstan Suspends Major Oil Exports as Black Sea Risks Escalate
item.description: Following a drone attack on the Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC) terminal along the Black Sea Coast, Kazakhstan has newly confirmed a halt to crude transfers there. "Kazakhstan is set to stop piping crude to a port on…

item.title: India Pulls Back From Iraqi Oil as Hormuz Turns Too Dangerous
item.description: India's biggest state refiners are no longer willing to gamble on the Strait of Hormuz. Indian Oil Corp. and Mangalore Refinery & Petrochemicals have suspended crude loadings from Iraq after a string of attacks on…

item.title: Inside Washington’s Quiet Bid to Break China’s Grip on Iraq
item.description: It is true that U.S. foreign policy under President Donald Trump now operates from the starting point of three core geographical spheres, dominated by a major power in each, as delineated in the ‘2025 National Security…

item.title: ADNOC approves $6.2bn Umm Shaif gas cap development
item.description: ADNOC has confirmed a Dh22.6bn ($6.2bn) final investment decision (FID) to develop the Umm Shaif Gas Cap in Abu Dhabi, UAE.

item.title: U.S. Says China Has Slashed Iranian Oil Purchases by 40%
item.description: The United States says its campaign to choke off Iran's oil revenue may finally be showing results where it matters most: China. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Tuesday that Chinese purchases of Iranian crude have…

item.title: Why Kyrgyz Citizens Are Turning Against China's Growing Footprint
item.description: China's expanding role in Kyrgyzstan has become increasingly hard to ignore. During the first half of 2026, Kyrgyzstan issued some 61,000 work visas, according to the Foreign Ministry's Consular Department, with about…

item.title: Houthi Threats Ignite New Oil Price Surge
item.description: Houthi attacks are expanding the Middle East conflict into the Red Sea, forcing tankers to reroute and pushing Brent above $91 amid growing fears of prolonged supply disruptions. Aramco Floods the Red Sea With Crude…

item.title: Ansaldo returns to US market with Texas gas turbine order
item.description: Ansaldo Energia has secured a contract to supply eight gas turbines for a Texas data centre power project, marking its return to US new-build generation.

item.title: OMV Petrom completes Neptun Deep platform installation in Black Sea
item.description: OMV Petrom, in partnership with S.N.G.N. ROMGAZ, has completed installation of the offshore production platform for the Neptun Deep gas project in the Black Sea.

item.title: Halliburton wins Basra Oil contract for southern Iraq field services
item.description: Halliburton has secured a contract from Basra Oil Company (BOC) to deliver Integrated Field Management Services (IFMS) for the development of the Bin Umar and Sindbad oil and gas fields located in southern Iraq.
